summary: Waldeyer's lymphatic chain includes the palatine, pharyngeal, tubarial, and lingual tonsils, forming a protective ring at the entry sites of the digestive and respiratory tracts. The post-auricular nodes are not part of this chain since they drain skin and skull regions unrelated to oral/pharyngeal entrances.
